title: Tithymalus schimperianus
taxonomy: Tithymalus schimperianus is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae and is a species of spurge. It belongs to the genus Tithymalus, which is now often considered a synonym for Euphorbia. Therefore, it can also be classified as Euphorbia schimperiana. The classification of Tithymalus schimperianus varies depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Euphorbiaceae family. Within the broader context of plant taxonomy, Euphorbiaceae is part of the order Malpighiales.

The plant's sap has also been used as an antidote for snake bites and other poisonous stings. Traditionally, Tithymalus schimperianus has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
